description: Target distribution of schema.org types in training data. Weights represent relative importance — the pipeline should aim to generate examples proportional to these weights. Total doesn't need to sum to 100.

LocalBusiness.notes: Largest category for Irish/UK SMEs. Ensure diverse subtypes.

Product.notes: E-commerce product pages with Offer. Include varied price formats, availability states.

Article.notes: Blog posts, news articles. Include author, publisher, dates.

Event.notes: Events with dates, venues, ticket prices. Include both upcoming and past events.

FAQPage.notes: FAQ sections common on service/product pages. Varies from 3 to 20+ questions.

Recipe.notes: Food blogs, recipe sites. Include ingredients, steps, nutrition, times.

Organization.notes: About pages, corporate sites. Include contact, social profiles.

Service.notes: Service pages for businesses. Often combined with LocalBusiness.

WebSite.notes: Homepage markup with SearchAction. Usually combined with other types.

BreadcrumbList.notes: Navigation breadcrumbs. Usually generated alongside primary type.

JobPosting.notes: Job listings with salary, location, requirements.

HowTo.notes: Step-by-step guides. Similar to Recipe but for non-food tasks.

multi_type_combinations.description: Many real pages need multiple schema types. At least 30% of training examples should contain 2+ types.
